verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass transmembrane protein resident in the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) membrane under baseline conditions. However, upon ER Ca2+ depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM contact sites (junctions), where it directly interacts with and activates the PM-resident ORAI1 calcium channel. The protein displays a documented, functionally essential PM pool at these junctions during store-operated Ca2+ entry (SOCE). The ER-luminal EF-hand domain senses Ca2+ depletion; the cytoplasmic C-terminus contains the ORAI-binding domain and reaches the plasma membrane during activation. STIM1 protein body is physically present on the outer face of the PM (or more precisely, at tight ER-PM junctions where it couples ER Ca2+ sensor signaling to PM channel gating). This is a canonical example of dual localization with activation-induced ER-PM trafficking and a functionally essential PM pool.
